response:
to solve this problem you need to process the given sorted array and compute the required values for each element. since the array is sorted in non-decreasing order you can use this property to efficiently calculate the sum of absolute differences. for each element at index i you can calculate the sum of absolute differences between nums[i] and all other elements. however for every third element you need to calculate the sum of squares instead of absolute differences. this can be done by iterating through the array and checking if the index is a multiple of three. for the sum of absolute differences you can take advantage of the sorted nature of the array to avoid unnecessary computations. for the sum of squares you can directly compute the square of the difference between nums[i] and each element in the array.

*** 

you can optimize the solution by precomputing prefix sums to quickly calculate the sum of absolute differences. for example the sum of absolute differences for nums[i] can be calculated using the prefix sum up to i and the suffix sum from i. this reduces the time complexity significantly. for the elements that are multiples of three you need to calculate the sum of squares which is straightforward but may be more computationally intensive. ensure that the output array is built correctly with the appropriate values for each index. remember to check for the condition that the index is a multiple of three and apply the square calculation accordingly. this approach ensures that the solution is efficient and meets the problem constraints.
